metadata.title: Vectra Detection Timeline Link
metadata.description: This playbook will trigger when a Vectra RUX incident is created. It queries the Detections data to resolve the Detection ID and Vectra URL for the incident's detection and entity, then posts a comment to the incident containing a Vectra pivot link and a link to the Vectra Detection Timeline workbook for quick investigation.
prerequisites: 1. The Vectra XDR data connector should be configured to create alerts and generate an incident based on entity data in Microsoft Sentinel.

2. The Vectra Detection Timeline workbook should be deployed. Obtain its full ARM resource ID to provide as the WorkbookResourceId parameter. Example: /subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Insights/workbooks/{guid}

3. Obtain the Log Analytics workspace name and workspace GUID (customerId) in which the Vectra XDR data connector is deployed. The workspace GUID can be retrieved with: az monitor log-analytics workspace show --resource-group <rg> --workspace-name <workspace> --query customerId -o tsv
postDeployment: **a. Authorize connections**
Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection.
1. Go to your logic app → API connections → Select azuresentinel connection resource
2. Go to General → edit API connection
3. Click Authorize
4. Sign in
5. Click Save
6. Repeat steps for other connections
**b. Configurations in Microsoft Sentinel**
1. In Microsoft Sentinel, configure an automation rule to trigger the playbook on incident creation.
a. Go to Microsoft Sentinel → <your workspace> → Automation
b. Click on Create → Automation rule
c. Provide a name for your rule
d. In the trigger, select 'When incident is created'
e. In the condition, filter by Analytic rule name containing 'Vectra RUX'
f. In Actions dropdown select Run playbook
g. In second dropdown select this deployed playbook
h. Click on Apply
i. Save the Automation rule.
NOTE: If you want to manually run the playbook on a particular incident follow the below steps:
a. Go to Microsoft Sentinel → <your workspace> → Incidents
b. Select an incident.
c. In the right pane, click on Actions, and from the dropdown select the 'Run Playbook' option.
d. click on the Run button beside this playbook.

metadata.description: Full ARM resource ID of the Vectra Detection Timeline workbook. Example: /subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Insights/workbooks/{guid}
Log Analytics workspace GUID (customerId). Get it with: az monitor log-analytics workspace show --resource-group rg-ccf-dev --workspace-name la-ccf-dev --query customerId -o tsv
Azure Portal URL for the Vectra Detection Timeline workbook.

body.query: @{concat('Detections_Data_CCF_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) | where d_type_vname has ''', outputs('Get_detection_name'), ''' and entity_name has ''', outputs('Get_entity_name'), ''' | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by detection_id | project DetectionID = tostring(toint(detection_id)), VectraURL = tostring(detection_href) | take 1')}

body.message:
@{concat('

🔍 Vectra Detection

','','','',if(empty(outputs('Get_detection_id')),'',concat('')),'
Detection',outputs('Get_detection_name'),'
Entity',outputs('Get_entity_name'),'
Detection ID',outputs('Get_detection_id'),'
',if(and(not(empty(outputs('Get_vectra_url'))),not(equals(outputs('Get_vectra_url'),'null'))),concat('

🔗 Open detection in Vectra AI

',''),''),concat('

📊 Open Detection Timeline Workbook

'),if(empty(outputs('Get_detection_id')),concat('

ℹ️ Detection ID not retrieved automatically. In the workbook, locate ',outputs('Get_detection_name'),' in the Detection Summary and click the row.

'),concat('

In the workbook, type ',outputs('Get_detection_id'),' into the Detection ID field.

')))}

PostDeploymentNote.value: Create a Sentinel Automation Rule: trigger = 'When incident is created', filter by analytics rule name containing 'Vectra RUX', action = run this playbook.
